In the agency admin section, when clicking the [remove configuration] button in the 'custom domains' section we get the following error: Error
Could not find the 'cloudflare_account_id' column of 'agency_branding' in the schema cache

The system admin > custom domains > configure your server section allows us to specify a domain name or an ip address. We used to have a hardcoded value from the lovable domain but we should acutally be using for an ip address whatever is givin in the 'server address' field and using that.
hide, don't remove the 'connect to cloudflare' panel in the custom domains setup, also remove connect to cloudflare from the 'system setup' section.

We have an issue where the system admin custom domain config seems to get confused when we save the settings. the [load balanacer ip address] has nothing to do with the custom app domain or the server ip address as the loal balancer ip is for the database. right now when we save we are getting the following error: DNS Misconfigured
agentace.cloud points to 98.84.224.111, but should point to 159.203.150.146

we need to update this query because we are missing the status fields in the tables. So create a table update script that will add all the status fields to the tables listed in this script.\
\
-- Diagnostic and Fix Script for PGRST200 Foreign Key Relationship Error
-- This script helps diagnose and fix the PostgREST relationship error between agencies and brand_themes

-- Step 1: Check current constraints on agencies table
SELECT
    tc.table_name,
    tc.constraint_name,
    tc.constraint_type,
    kcu.column_name,
    ccu.table_name AS foreign_table_name,
    ccu.column_name AS foreign_column_name
FROM information_schema.table_constraints AS tc
JOIN information_schema.key_column_usage AS kcu
    ON tc.constraint_name = kcu.constraint_name
    AND tc.table_schema = kcu.table_schema
JOIN information_schema.constraint_column_usage AS ccu
    ON ccu.constraint_name = tc.constraint_name
    AND ccu.table_schema = tc.table_schema
WHERE tc.table_name = 'agencies'
    AND tc.table_schema = 'public'
    AND tc.constraint_type = 'FOREIGN KEY'
ORDER BY tc.constraint_name;

-- Step 2: Check all tables that reference agencies
SELECT
    tc.table_name,
    tc.constraint_name,
    kcu.column_name,
    ccu.table_name AS referenced_table_name,
    ccu.column_name AS referenced_column_name
FROM information_schema.table_constraints AS tc
JOIN information_schema.key_column_usage AS kcu
    ON tc.constraint_name = kcu.constraint_name
    AND tc.table_schema = kcu.table_schema
JOIN information_schema.constraint_column_usage AS ccu
    ON ccu.constraint_name = tc.constraint_name
    AND ccu.table_schema = tc.table_schema
WHERE ccu.table_name = 'agencies'
    AND tc.table_schema = 'public'
    AND tc.constraint_type = 'FOREIGN KEY'
ORDER BY tc.table_name, tc.constraint_name;

-- Step 3: Check if brand_themes table exists and its structure
SELECT
    column_name,
    data_type,
    is_nullable,
    column_default
FROM information_schema.columns
WHERE table_name = 'brand_themes'
    AND table_schema = 'public'
ORDER BY ordinal_position;

-- Step 4: Check if branding_settings table exists and its structure
SELECT
    column_name,
    data_type,
    is_nullable,
    column_default
FROM information_schema.columns
WHERE table_name = 'branding_settings'
    AND table_schema = 'public'
ORDER BY ordinal_position;

-- Step 5: Check agencies table structure
SELECT
    column_name,
    data_type,
    is_nullable,
    column_default
FROM information_schema.columns
WHERE table_name = 'agencies'
    AND table_schema = 'public'
ORDER BY ordinal_position;

-- Step 6: Check for any orphaned or incorrect constraints
SELECT
    tc.constraint_name,
    tc.table_name,
    kcu.column_name,
    rc.unique_constraint_name,
    rc.update_rule,
    rc.delete_rule
FROM information_schema.table_constraints tc
JOIN information_schema.referential_constraints rc
    ON tc.constraint_name = rc.constraint_name
    AND tc.constraint_schema = rc.constraint_schema
JOIN information_schema.key_column_usage kcu
    ON tc.constraint_name = kcu.constraint_name
    AND tc.constraint_schema = kcu.constraint_schema
WHERE tc.constraint_type = 'FOREIGN KEY'
    AND (tc.table_name = 'agencies' OR rc.unique_constraint_catalog LIKE '%branding%')
    AND tc.table_schema = 'public'
ORDER BY tc.table_name, tc.constraint_name;

-- Step 7: Refresh PostgREST schema cache (run this after applying fixes)
-- This will be executed as a separate command
-- NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';
